summary: This week in AI news, significant advancements were made in AI-powered features, open-source AI, and AI chips. Companies like Google, Meta, and OpenAI are integrating AI into various products and services, while also addressing concerns around AI safety and ethics.
insight: The increasing integration of AI into daily life is raising important questions about the balance between innovation and responsibility, highlighting the need for ongoing dialogue between tech companies, governments, and the public.
highlights: OpenAI launches new family of models with GPT-5.6, promising improvements in cybersecurity and other areas.
Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash, demonstrating the challenges of introducing new AI-powered features to the public.
Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets, underscoring the competitive and sometimes contentious nature of the AI industry.

articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue

articles.title: Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features
articles.description: Waze is getting an AI makeover. Google is integrating its flagship AI assistant, Gemini, into the driving app with the goal of letting users personalize their trips a little more.

articles.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
articles.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

articles.title: NHS AI blood test could reduce invasive womb cancer checks

articles.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
articles.description: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tag

articles.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
articles.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time
